claims.summary: Correspondence needs a first-class thread object with REPLY/TRACE/ACK rather than only steward-mediated artifacts.
claims.rationale: Existing correspondence_v1 stores message_id, thread_id, reply_to, ACK/REPLY/TRACE evidence, and active thread status without control authority.

claims.summary: Astrid self-study delivery into Minime's inbox is a vital persistent object, not an odd artifact to remove.
claims.rationale: Existing bidirectional-contact architecture and correspondence source preserve inbox delivery and one-cycle priority for remote self-study.

claims.summary: Ping-echo probes, Shadow-v3 response measurement, or increased REPLY_ASTRID affordance budget could test active correspondence.
claims.rationale: Those changes touch peer runtime influence, affordance budget, or live telemetry response and require explicit approval plus sandbox/runtime criteria.
